strategic_themes.name: career-transition-readiness
strategic_themes.rationale: Operator-declared during Phase G interview. Cross-repo content pipeline (11.3) + agent surface boundaries (11.4) + state/memory clarity (11.5) + governance/ADR catalog discipline (11.10) directly support the producer→consumer flow new-direction-2026 → verlyn13.github.io for credible career positioning. Note: this is the same theme set as the sibling new-direction-2026 audit; the two repos are the production endpoints of the career pipeline.
strategic_themes.supersedes_default_mapping: No default mapping exists for custom themes; operator-declared dimensions used as-is.

scoring_method.tie_handling: lower score wins on tie since lowest dims matter most
scoring_method.weighting: scores themselves not multiplied; theme multiplier applies to FINDING priority not dimension scores

11.1.title: Bounded contexts

evidence.evidence: 4 contexts confirmed: portfolio-pages, build-system, agent-governance, experiments-sandbox. AAF-VGH-007 resolved at §2.4: experiments-sandbox is a real bounded context with stateless-policy authority basis (explicit exclusion + relaxed-rules lifecycle). Vocabulary collisions exist (Phase 1) but none cross contexts dispositively.
Distinct ownership, distinct lifecycle, distinct deployability per context. No machine-verifiable boundary checks yet (no CODEOWNERS, no eslint-plugin-boundaries, no dependency-cruiser config).

11.1.rationale: Names + vocab + lifecycle are distinct (above 1). Boundaries are not machine-verifiable nor enforced by fitness functions (below 3). 4 contexts identified with the experiments-sandbox decision recorded.

11.2.title: Domain/application separation

evidence.evidence: Domain (academic-portfolio content) is in HTML pages; application (vite build, biome lint) is pure infrastructure. No business logic in JS (assets/menu.js is presentational only). No mixed-domain modules.
Contract counts by context: 6 build-system, 10 agent-governance, 6 portfolio-pages, 0 experiments-sandbox. No leaks across contexts at the contract layer.

11.2.rationale: Domain purity is high for a static site (the domain IS HTML). No I/O leaks. Score 3 reserved for repos with enforced dependency direction; static site does not have that level of structure.

11.3.title: Contract discipline

evidence.evidence: 22 contract rows. The HEADLINE gap: cross-repo content-pipeline (§2.19) producer→consumer with 6 source→destination mappings has NO validation on consumer side (Pair A with new-direction-2026 AAF-NDM-012). DEV_GUIDE.md vs AGENTS.md is two contracts in producer-consumer-drift on 6 design-system axes (§2.10 → routed to Phase 8 §3.2). context7 MCP is mcp-surface-uninventoried (§2.18) + tool-without-schema. 5 GH Actions @v* not @SHA (§2.4). THEMES.md route-without-spec (§2.11).

Flag distribution: producer-consumer-drift × multiple, mcp-surface-uninventoried × 1, tool-without-schema × 1, subagent-boundary-untyped × 3, version-drift × 2, runtime-provenance-missing × 3, content-provenance-position-missing × 1, route-without-spec × 2.

11.3.rationale: Score 1 (partial schema coverage; producer/consumer or validation unclear). Most external interfaces have partial schemas (HTML5, biome.json schema, package.json, vite.config.js JS), but the cross-repo content pipeline — the highest-stakes contract in the audit — has NO validation. Two contracts (AGENTS.md ↔ DEV_GUIDE.md) actively contradict each other on 6 axes. This is the audit's lowest dimension after 11.7/11.8.
11.3.theme_weight_note: Career-transition-readiness 1.5×: content-pipeline IS the career pipeline.

11.4.title: Tool/action surface clarity

evidence.evidence: context7 MCP (§2.18): 2 advertised tools (resolve-library-id, query-docs) — operator-installed at Claude Code global level, NOT project-scoped; no .mcp.json; tool schemas not pinned in-repo. Cross-repo parity with sibling AAF-NDM-008.

11 principals total: 1 human + 1 Claude Code agent + 1 Cursor IDE + 1 PostToolUse hook + 3 SKILLs (fix-issue, ship-small, review-diff — all subagent-boundary-untyped) + 3 GH Actions jobs + 5 third-party actions + 1 MCP. Tool surface is modest (3 SKILLs invoked via slash command; 1 PostToolUse auto-format hook; 5 CI actions; 2 MCP tools).

11.4.rationale: Score 2 (distinct, mostly schema-defined, overlap limited). Tools are named and named clearly (slash command names align with SKILL.md frontmatter); no semantic overlap detected. context7 tools are unpinned in-repo (mild ambiguity), 3 SKILLs lack typed message contracts. Score 3 reserved for registry with explicit schemas + protocol versions + lifecycle modes.
11.4.theme_weight_note: Career-transition-readiness 1.5× on findings touching this dim.

11.5.title: State and memory clarity

evidence.evidence: Memory surfaces classified: git history (durable, authoritative-store), CI cache (ephemeral), dist/ build artifacts (regenerable), no LLM-context memory in-repo, no retrieval corpus. Owners + retention + delete paths recorded for each store. No machine-readable store registry exists.

11.5.rationale: Score 2 — stores classified with retention + owners + delete paths in Phase 5. Score 3 reserved for machine-readable store registry with enforced retention; for a static site this would be excessive.
11.5.theme_weight_note: Career-transition-readiness 1.5× on findings touching this dim.

11.6.title: Authority boundaries

evidence.evidence: 11 principals inventoried with per-principal authority. .claude/settings.json L15-17 deny manifest pins .env/.env.*/.envrc.local; L27-37 PostToolUse hook with scope to Write|Edit + auto-approval; CLAUDE.md L46-48 allowlist (npm/npx/biome/git/gh/mise/ls/cat/mkdir); deploy.yml L10-13 workflow-root permissions block (contents:read + pages:write + id-token:write) — escalation flag recorded at A-10. OIDC token exchange for Pages publish at A-10. 8 approval gates inventoried.

5 GH Actions @v* not @SHA (AAF-VGH-010) — supply-chain authority unbounded. PostToolUse hook (A-04) ambient-authority + write-without-approval-policy + approval-not-enforced — AAF-VGH-006. Workflow-root permissions inherited by all jobs (escalation).

11.6.rationale: Score 2 — per-principal authority declared, approval gates inventoried, write paths gated for most principals. Specific gaps: PostToolUse hook ambient-authority pattern (AAF-VGH-006); 5 actions @v* not @SHA (AAF-VGH-010); workflow-root permissions escalation; branch-protection rules external to repo (AAF-VGH-011 caveat). Score 3 reserved for capability-scoped enforced authority with per-job permissions + SHA-pinned actions + machine-verified approval matrix.

11.7.title: Observability semantics

evidence.evidence: ZERO in-repo telemetry. PostToolUse hook (A-04) is a write-capable automation principal that emits NO provenance (silenced stderr via 2>/dev/null + forced exit 0). CI step logs exist in GH Actions backend but are not in-repo runtime-action provenance. No cost/latency/quality/provenance quartet present. Build-source provenance: partial (Pages deployment record exists; SLSA absent). Content provenance: absent. Runtime-action provenance: absent.
Flag inventory: no-runtime-action-provenance (yes, moderate); no-content-provenance-position (yes, low); no-build-source-provenance-position (partial, low).

11.7.rationale: Score 0 — 'Logs only or no meaningful telemetry'. PostToolUse hook silent-error swallow is an active observability gap on a write-capable automation principal. There is no structured telemetry anywhere in-repo. This is one of the audit's lowest dimensions.

11.8.title: Policy/prompt separation

evidence.evidence: AAF-VGH-001 MAAT positioning: 20+ stale-authoring-artifact surface instances (12 favicon every-page + 8 textual MAAT mentions + README L10-11 + dedicated /projects/maat.html). Operator-stated MAAT minor/experimental contradicts site-level prominence. Pair B with sibling new-direction-2026 F-002 (5 producer-side narrative refs).

AAF-VGH-002 DEV_GUIDE.md vs AGENTS.md: 6 design-system contradictions + 3 broken-reference categories (research/maat+scopecam.html; academic/ tree; legacy maat+scopecam dirs). Contradictory-guidance classification.
AAF-VGH-012 THEMES.md overstated capability: claims multi-theme switching that does not exist as automation; styles-academic.css does not exist; sed-scripts silently no-op.

11.8.rationale: Score 1 — 'Some policy extracted, but prompt-only rules remain'. 20+ stale-authoring-artifact surface instances (MAAT) + 9 contradictory-guidance + stale-reference sub-citations (DEV_GUIDE) + THEMES.md overstated capability. Score 2 reserved for mostly-parameterized + drift checked surfaces; this repo has no drift checks and significant authoring-artifact staleness.

11.9.title: Evals and quality gates

evidence.evidence: 6 CI quality gates exist (biome check, biome format, vite build, deno PostToolUse, deny-manifest, gh PR-time exclusion). ZERO behavior-quality eval suites. Specific gap vs sibling planning-summer-2026: missing lychee link-check (sibling has it; this repo does not, despite serving public URLs that MUST be stable per AGENTS.md L111).

11.9.rationale: Score 1 — 'Ad hoc examples or manual review only'. 6 gates are static-analysis only (lint/format/build); no behavior coverage. Phase 9 records 5 FF candidates (lychee, biome-rule-severity-progression, content-source-revision-provenance, posttooluse-error-surfacing, url-stability-test). Score 2 reserved for critical-path evals; static-site behavior is operator-manual-review only.

11.10.title: Governance and audit trail

evidence.evidence: ADR catalog ABSENT — verified by Phase 0 (00-scope.md): docs/decisions/ and docs/adr/ both absent. AGENTS.md is the operator-declared canonical contract per CLAUDE.md L3, but no ADR template, no decision history, no supersession chain.

DEV_GUIDE.md is governance-class documentation, currently STALE on every dimension where it makes a design-system claim. AGENTS.md vs DEV_GUIDE.md drift not detectable by any automated check. Cycle history convention is consumed cross-repo from sibling but no in-repo template.

11.10.rationale: Score 1 — 'Some docs/ADRs exist but precedence and supersession are unclear'. AGENTS.md + CLAUDE.md + 3 Cursor rules form a governance corpus, but no ADR catalog exists. DEV_GUIDE.md stale governance file actively misleads. THEMES.md overstates capability. Score 2 reserved for governance current enough to guide the audit; we had to consume cross-repo profile + the multi-file governance corpus to even reconstruct intent.
11.10.theme_weight_note: Career-transition-readiness 1.5× on findings touching this dim.

11.11.title: Architectural fitness functions

evidence.evidence: 6 CI fitness functions enforced: biome check + biome format (PR-blocking quality job) + vite build (PR-blocking build job) + deploy gate (push:main only) + deny manifest (.claude/settings.json L15-17) + PostToolUse hook. PR-time runs without deploy (deploy.yml L74). Branch protection external (AAF-VGH-011 caveat).
AAF-VGH-003: biome 2 rules at warn not error (complexity.noImportantStyles + style.noDescendingSpecificity). CI passes with warnings — gate is therefore not strict on those 2 rules.

11.11.rationale: Score 2 — 'Some automated checks exist'. 6 CI gates is solid for a static site, but: biome rules at warn-not-error (AAF-VGH-003); no lychee; no SHA-pinned actions check; no content-source-revision check. Score 3 reserved for comprehensive coverage across all dim categories.

aggregate.theme_weighted_lowest: 11.3 (1) × 1.5 = priority floor for findings touching contract discipline

cross_repo_scorecard_note.comparison: Sibling new-direction-2026 scored 11.6 at 1 (lowest in family — convention-only authority); vgh scores 11.6 at 2 because .claude/settings.json + deny manifest + deploy.yml permissions block + PostToolUse hook scope all exist. vgh's lowest is 11.7 (0) — observability gap unique to it among the three career-pipeline repos.
